name: tenant-a
description: Well-configured SMB tenant. MFA covers all users with break-glass exclusion, legacy auth blocked, admin MFA enforced, sign-in and user risk policies in place, guest restrictions on untrusted countries, and compliant device required for sensitive apps.
notes: calibrated against rules engine v0.0.1; all findings are LOW or MEDIUM hygiene observations, no CRITICAL or HIGH gaps.

signin_scenarios.name: baseline
signin_scenarios.description: 30 days of normal behaviour, no injected anomalies.

signin_scenarios.name: replay-attacks
signin_scenarios.description: Baseline plus scripted impossible-travel, MFA bypass, off-hours admin, credential stuffing, new ASN.

signin_scenarios.name: noisy
signin_scenarios.description: Operationally noisy normals plus the same scripted attacks.

anomaly_calibration.pipeline: per-user IsolationForest(n=200, random_state=42) + cold-start pooled IF + 4 hybrid rules + off-hours-admin rule
anomaly_calibration.notes: Sprint 3.5 architecture: per-user IF lifts coverage of user-specific patterns (e.g. off-hours admin signin caught via per-user time anchor) but tenant-a has zero cold-start users so the pooled fallback is dormant on this fixture. Layer attribution: per-user IF alone catches 2/27 attacks; the four hybrid rules drive recall to 22/27; the off-hours-admin rule adds the 23rd. Precision sits below the Sprint 3.5 0.40 target because the hybrid rules trigger on legitimate-but-rare events (mobile-carrier ASN swaps, traveler sign-ins). Sprint 7 calibration against real tenant data is expected to yield more discriminating per-user models.
